dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3861 IS: Rural Innovation Stronger Economy (RISE) Reauthorization Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Welch (for himself and Mrs. Gillibrand) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry

form.official-title: To amend the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act to reauthorize and expand the Rural Innovation Stronger Economy grant program.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Rural Innovation Stronger Economy (RISE) Reauthorization Act of 2026.

section.header: Rural Innovation Stronger Economy grant program reauthorization and expansion

subsection.text: Section 379I of the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act (7 U.S.C. 2008w) is amended—

subclause.text: by striking the industry clusters that are objectively identified as; and
by inserting industries in the area served by the partnership after declining; and
by redesignating subclauses (II) through (IV) as subclauses (I) through (III), respectively;

subparagraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (3) through (5) as paragraphs (2) through (4), respectively;

clause.text: by redesignating subparagraphs (A) and (B) as clauses (i) and (ii), respectively, and indenting the clauses appropriately;
in clause (ii) (as so redesignated), by striking opportunities, networks, and industry clusters and inserting opportunities and networks;
by striking the paragraph designation and heading and all that follows through The Secretary in the matter preceding clause (i) (as so redesignated) and inserting the following:
by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: In carrying out the grant program under this paragraph, the Secretary shall—

clause.text: target a broad base of rural community types that will benefit from the program, with an emphasis on communities with a population of fewer than 20,000 inhabitants; and

of the total amount of grants awarded for each fiscal year, award not less than 10 percent to carry out activities that will benefit rural communities with a population of fewer than 10,000 inhabitants.

subclause.text: by redesignating clauses (i) and (ii) as subclauses (I) and (II), respectively, and indenting the subclauses appropriately; and
in subclause (II) (as so redesignated), by striking industry cluster;

clause.text: in subparagraph (D), by striking industry clusters,;
by redesignating subparagraphs (A), (B), (D), (E), and (F) as clauses (i), (ii), (iii), (iv), and (v), respectively, and indenting the clauses appropriately; and
in the matter preceding clause (i) (as so redesignated), by striking shall consider— and inserting the following:

subparagraph.text: ensure that a diverse set of industry bases is represented;
only select an eligible entity to receive a grant with the concurrence of the applicable State office of the rural development mission area; and
in subparagraph (A), by adding and after the semicolon at the end; and

clause.text: in clause (i), by striking industry clusters,;
in clause (vii), by striking small businesses in regional industry clusters, including;
in clause (ix), by striking in the identified industry clusters; and
in clause (x), by striking industry cluster;
in subclause (I), by striking industry cluster and inserting activities funded with the grant; and
in subclause (II), by striking held by the industry cluster and inserting relating to those activities convened by relevant organizations;

subparagraph.text: in clauses (xi) and (xii), by striking industry cluster each place it appears and inserting participating regional; and
in clause (xiii), by striking the project activities and inserting activities funded with the grant; and

paragraph.text: by striking subsection (f) and inserting the following:

subsection.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.

Section 6306(h)(3)(B)(ii) of the Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018 (7 U.S.C. 2204b–3(h)(3)(B)(ii)) is amended, in the matter preceding subclause (I), by striking section 379I(a)(4) of the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act and inserting section 379I(a) of the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act (7 U.S.C. 2008w(a)).
